title: Identified root cause of outdated test images
decision: The current debug-boot.sh uses Ubuntu 18.04.5 LTS (bionic) from 2018, which reached EOL in April 2023. This explains the apt-daily service failures and general staleness.

title: Upgrade to official Firecracker CI Ubuntu 24.04 images
decision: Use official Firecracker CI images from s3://spec.ccfc.min/firecracker-ci/20251218-f2f293f67e5f-0/x86_64/ which includes Ubuntu 24.04 squashfs and kernel 6.1.155

alternatives_considered.rejected_because: Unnecessary complexity when official images exist
24.04 available and aligns with CLAUDE.md target

chosen_option: Use official Firecracker CI Ubuntu 24.04 + kernel 6.1.155
rationale: Official images are tested by Firecracker team, recent (Dec 2025), and align with project target OS

title: Select kernel 6.1.155 over 5.10.245
decision: Use kernel 6.1.155 for modern features and longer support window

alternatives_considered.pros: Newer LTS, better hardware support, longer EOL

rationale: 6.1 is the newer LTS kernel with support until 2028, better for a new project starting in 2025

title: Convert squashfs to ext4 for writable rootfs
decision: Download Ubuntu 24.04 squashfs and convert to ext4 for Firecracker compatibility (requires read-write root)

technical_details.conversion_method: unsquashfs to tempdir, then create ext4 and copy contents

title: Document VM exit mechanisms
decision: Document multiple exit methods: Ctrl+C (SIGINT to firecracker process), 'reboot' command in guest, 'poweroff' in guest
problem: User was stuck in VM without clear exit path
solution: Ctrl+C should work since debug-boot.sh runs firecracker in foreground. If not, use separate terminal: pkill firecracker

title: Update fixture management for modern images
decision: Create download-fixtures.sh script that fetches latest Firecracker CI images and converts to usable format
rationale: Avoid committing 300MB+ binaries to git, enable reproducible builds

title: Added NO STALE IMAGES rule to CLAUDE.md
decision: Added Section 11 to CLAUDE.md making it a permanent, mandatory rule to never use EOL or outdated base images
trigger: Ubuntu 18.04 incident causing debug session failures
scope: All future development - applies to OS images, kernels, container images, test fixtures

title: Ubuntu 24.04 boot successful
decision: Confirmed Ubuntu 24.04 + kernel 6.1.155 boots correctly via debug-boot.sh

title: Fix root=/dev/vda1 to root=/dev/vda
decision: Change kernel args from root=/dev/vda1 to root=/dev/vda because Firecracker CI rootfs images are unpartitioned ext4 filesystems, not disk images with partition tables

title: Firecracker CI kernel 6.1.155 missing virtio_blk
decision: Use quickstart kernel (4.14.174) instead of CI kernel (6.1.155) because CI kernel lacks virtio_blk support needed for block devices
evidence: CI kernel boots but kernel panic with unknown-block(0,0). Quickstart kernel boots Ubuntu 24.04 successfully.
action: Update vmlinux.bin symlink to point to vmlinux-quickstart

title: Use 5.10.245-no-acpi kernel for ext4 rootfs
decision: Use vmlinux-5.10.245-no-acpi (Dec 2025, LTS until 2026) instead of 6.1.155 or old 4.14.174
rationale: CI kernels with ACPI require CONFIG_PCI which is only in Amazon Linux patches. The -no-acpi variant uses legacy MPTable boot that works with virtio-mmio block devices.

title: Root cause: Firecracker CI kernels require Amazon Linux patches
decision: CI kernels (5.10.245, 6.1.155) with ACPI enabled require CONFIG_PCI=y which is only available in Amazon Linux microvm kernel patches. Standard mainline kernels fail with 'unknown-block(0,0)' because virtio-mmio devices cannot initialize without proper ACPI+PCI support.

title: Final kernel choice: 5.10.245-no-acpi
decision: Use vmlinux-5.10.245-no-acpi from Firecracker CI (Dec 2025). This kernel uses legacy MPTable boot instead of ACPI, avoiding the CONFIG_PCI requirement. LTS support until Sept 2026.

alternatives_rejected.reason: Requires ACPI+PCI, fails without Amazon Linux patches

title: Final approved stack for nanofuse debug/test
decision: Ubuntu 24.04 rootfs (EOL 2029) + kernel 5.10.245-no-acpi (EOL Sept 2026). Both components are current, supported, and tested working together.

title: Made register-local-image resilient and idempotent
decision: Fixed register-local-image.go to be reproducible: (1) Added UpsertImage method to storage/db.go with ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for idempotent operations, (2) Added symlink resolution with filepath.EvalSymlinks so resolved paths are stored, (3) Added kernel version extraction from filename using regex. Scripts can now be run multiple times without failure.
trigger: UNIQUE constraint failed error on re-registration attempt

title: Debug stack fully validated
decision: Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS + kernel 5.10.245-no-acpi successfully boots via nanofuse. VM auto-logs in as root. Full debug environment operational.
